title: Memory-V2V: Memory-Augmented Video-to-Video Diffusion for Consistent Multi-Turn Editing
evidence_location: PDF text cache, no reliable page map

high_rated_dimensions.reason: The paper directly studies iterative generative editing and cross-turn consistency, which is close to process-level generation and editing workflows.
It explicitly formulates multi-turn video editing as a distinct setting where prior edits constrain later generations.
The method combines task-aware retrieval, dynamic tokenization, and adaptive token merging to use memory without linear context growth.
The paper positions itself against ReCamMaster, LucyEdit, and long-video memory/context methods.
The paper treats editing as a multi-turn process rather than a single isolated generation.
It reframes memory in video editing as structured constraints across independently denoised edits.

risk_or_uncertainty: Quick-read relies on extracted text; exact quantitative table values and ablation details require layout/table verification or deep reading.
